mentions.raw_name: A new young lord in Darry
Brienne's father (Lord Selwyn Tarth, not named)
Brienne of Tarth (Brienne, Lady Brienne, the Maid of Tarth)
A new young lord in Darry (unnamed)
Brienne's father (unnamed, Lord Selwyn Tarth implied)
House Stark (Stark — mentioned in context of dead buried on isle and Sansa/Arya)
House Lannister (Lannister — mentioned in context of dead buried on isle; "the lions" who hanged Masha Heddle)
House Frey (dead buried on isle; Walder Frey hunting outlaws)
House Darry (dead buried on isle; new lord at Darry)
The Quiet Isle brotherhood (penitent brothers under vows of silence)
The Brave Companions (Bloody Mummers — Timeon and Vargo Hoat were members)
The outlaws/raiders at Saltpans (unnamed band wearing the Hound's helm)
Renly's Rainbow Guard (Brienne wore the rainbow cloak)

The Faith of the Seven (the Seven Above; the Father, the Mother, the Smith specifically named; the seven-sided steeple; The Seven-Pointed Star read aloud; vows of silence as devotion; the sept with doors carved with Mother and Father; prayer before meals; confession)
Dornishmen (Timeon called "the Dornishman"; Prince Oberyn "of Dorne")
Fisherfolk (of Saltpans, who fled to Maidenpool)
Oathkeeper (Brienne's Valyrian steel sword, named by Jaime)
Rhaegar's rubies (six found on the Quiet Isle, seventh awaited)
The Hound's helm (placed on Clegane's cairn, stolen by a wayfarer)
Driftwood furniture (cups, table, settle, chest, chairs — all in the Hermit's Hole)
The rainbow cloak (Brienne's from Renly's guard)
Silver cups, iron pots, sacks of wool, bolts of silk, rusted helms, shining swords (items washed up on the isle)
The Seven-Pointed Star (read aloud by proctors during supper)
mentions.section: Raw Entity List > In-world Texts & Songs

mentions.raw_name: The shadow in the king's pavilion (referenced in Brienne's outpouring — the shadow that killed Renly)
The Battle of the Trident (Elder Brother fought and "died" there; Rhaegar vs. Robert; rubies from Rhaegar's armor)
Robert's Rebellion (implied — the Battle of the Trident is part of it; "the dragon rather than the stag")
The War of the Five Kings (implied — "the wars are ending"; corpses of Stark, Lannister, Blackwood, Bracken, Frey, Darry washing up; general devastation of the riverlands)
The sack of Saltpans (raiders wearing the Hound's helm burned the town, killed and raped townsfolk)
The mêlée at Bitterbridge (referenced in Brienne's outpouring)
Elder Brother (leader of the Quiet Isle)
Proctor (speaking brothers of the order — one day in seven)
Knight (Ser Hyle Hunt; Ser Quincy Cox; the Elder Brother formerly; Brienne called "warrior maid")
Lord (Renly; Walder Frey; new lord in Darry; Brienne's father)
